raw_output:
Ooooh, that’s a really good question! It’s like… imagine you have a bouncy ball and you throw it at a wall. The bouncy part bounces off, right? Well, sunlight is kind of like that bouncy ball.

The sun makes *all* the colors – red, yellow, blue, everything! But when the light comes to Earth, it bumps into tiny bits in the air, like really, really small dust and stuff. 

Blue light is a bit like a super-fast bouncy ball. It bounces off those little bits *way* more than the other colors do. So, it spreads out all over the sky! That's why we see blue most of the time – because that’s the color bouncing around everywhere. 

It’s kinda weird, isn’t it?
